title: LinkedIn App Reviews: What App Store & Google Play Users Say
description: Star-by-star review data for LinkedIn: Community & Network, fetched 2026-07-12: 2.20 of 5 across 240 reviews, 18% 5-star.
answer_first: A July 12, 2026 scrape of 240 LinkedIn: Community & Network reviews puts the combined average at 2.20 of 5 - App Store 2.03, Google Play 3.02. The split runs from 44 five-star reviews (18%) down to 135 one-star (56%). Baseline capture 2026-07-12: 240 reviews recorded; rating trends begin with the next refresh. Below, every star level is tallied per store with counts and shares.
facts: The 2026-07-12 window of 240 reviews averages 2.20 of 5 - App Store 2.03, Google Play 3.02.
Five-star reviews make up 18% of the window (44 of 240).
1-star reviews account for 56% of the window - 135 of 240.
US contributes the most reviews (240) of the 1 country feed(s) sampled.
Keyword counting across the 160 negative (1-2 star) reviews flags login & accounts as the top complaint, appearing in 24 of them.
Baseline established 2026-07-12 with 240 reviews; trend facts start at the next refresh.

faq.q: Does this match the rating shown in the app stores?
faq.a: Not necessarily. This window covers 240 recent reviews from 2026-07-12, while store-front ratings average the app's whole history. Treat the 2.20 of 5 here as a current-sentiment reading, not a lifetime score.

faq.q: Where do these reviews come from?
faq.a: Directly from the public review feeds of the app stores, collected in one automated run on 2026-07-12. The window holds 240 reviews for LinkedIn: Community & Network across 2 store feed(s), tallied without edits.

faq.q: Do App Store and Google Play users score it differently?
faq.a: This capture shows App Store 2.03, Google Play 3.02 across 240 total reviews from 2026-07-12. Small per-store gaps are common in recent-review windows and can flip between refreshes.

faq.q: Does this work for other apps too?
faq.a: It does: point the source actor at any package name or App Store ID and it returns the identical structure - ratings, dates, text, helpful votes - that produced these 240 rows.
